deep_dive.title: HeadCast: Casting Attention Heads for Efficient Autoregressive Video Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Autoregressive (AR) video diffusion models have become a promising paradigm for long and streaming video synthesis, but the continuously growing Key-Value (KV) cache makes attention the dominant inference cost, especially at high resolution where each frame contributes many tokens. Existing remedies either evict the cache with coarse heuristics that cause inter-frame flickering, or require model re-training. We propose HeadCast, a training-free, plug-and-play acceleration framework built on the observation that a pre-trained AR model's attention heads exhibit stable, heterogeneous behaviors. After a short warm-up, HeadCast performs a one-time classification at the maximum-noise step that sorts every head into one of four archetypes: Sink, Dummy, Spatial, and Global, and restructures the monolithic KV cache into head-specific pathways. Crucially, it retains the Global heads that preserve the long-range temporal consistency aggressive eviction destroys. Because the Spatial pathway operates on a fixed-size grid, its savings grow with resolution: across state-of-the-art AR models, HeadCast accelerates inference by up to 1.62x at 720P and 1.95x at 1080P, while keeping VBench quality on par with full attention and largely flicker-free. Code is available at https://github.com/sjlgaga/HeadCast .
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: training-free plug-and-play acceleration framework for autoregressive video diffusion models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 免训练即插即用自回归视频扩散模型加速框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 免训练即插即用自回归视频扩散模型加速框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 免训练即插即用自回归视频扩散模型加速框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: HeadCast is a training-free, plug-and-play acceleration framework for autoregressive video generation that classifies attention heads into archetypes to reduce KV cache overhead without retraining.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 自回归视频扩散模型推理时KV缓存不断增长，导致注意力计算成为主要瓶颈。现有方法通常需要重训练或采用粗糙启发式缓存驱逐导致闪烁。本文提出HeadCast，一种免训练即插即用加速框架，通过预热后一次性分类注意力头为四种原型，在最大噪声步后精简缓存，显著提升推理速度且不牺牲质量。该方法直接适用于视频生成加速。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 自回归视频扩散模型推理时KV缓存不断增长，导致注意力计算成为主要瓶颈。现有方法通常需要重训练或采用粗糙启发式缓存驱逐导致闪烁。本文提出HeadCast，一种免训练即插即用加速框架，通过预热后一次性分类注意力头为四种原型，在最大噪声步后精简缓存，显著提升推理速度且不牺牲质量。该方法直接适用于视频生成加速。

deep_dive.title: Importance-Aware OBS Pruning for Diffusion Models
deep_dive.abstract: We propose importance-aware pruning for diffusion models, a training-free framework that prioritizes preserving parameters critical to semantically salient image regions. To do so, we incorporate spatial importance maps -- derived from conditioning signals or model attention -- into the pruning objective. This produces parameter rankings aligned with perceptual relevance rather than uniform reconstruction error. On MS-COCO dataset, our proposed approach consistently retains subject fidelity and structural correctness at high compression ratios where conventional pruning causes visible degradation. These results demonstrate that content-aware objectives are key to perceptually faithful compression of generative models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 免训练剪枝加速扩散模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 免训练剪枝加速扩散模型

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes importance-aware pruning for diffusion models, a training-free framework that preserves parameters critical to salient regions, achieving high compression without retraining.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 提出重要性感知剪枝框架，无需训练即可压缩扩散模型。通过融入基于注意力或条件信号的空间重要性图，在剪枝中优先保留对语义重要区域的关键参数。在MS-COCO上，该方法在高压缩比下仍能保持主体保真度和结构正确性，优于传统剪枝。表明内容感知目标对生成模型感知保真压缩至关重要。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 提出重要性感知剪枝框架，无需训练即可压缩扩散模型。通过融入基于注意力或条件信号的空间重要性图，在剪枝中优先保留对语义重要区域的关键参数。在MS-COCO上，该方法在高压缩比下仍能保持主体保真度和结构正确性，优于传统剪枝。表明内容感知目标对生成模型感知保真压缩至关重要。

deep_dive.title: OSVE: One Step Video Editing with One Step Diffusion Models
deep_dive.abstract: Text-guided video editing with diffusion models is impractically slow, hindered by costly multi-step sampling and inversion. We present OSVE, the first framework to successfully adapt one-step Text-to-Image (T2I) models for high-quality video editing, addressing the core challenges of inversion, editability, and temporal consistency. To bypass slow iterative inversion, we train a learnable encoder that predicts the initial noise for each frame in a single forward pass. This encoder is trained with a novel Structure-Aware Editing (SAE) loss on a curated dataset of structurally-aligned image pairs, teaching it to preserve the source video's geometry during edits. For temporal coherence, we introduce Unified-Frame Editing (UFE), a technique that concatenates frame latents to facilitate cross-frame attention in a single generation step. Furthermore, for long videos, a sliding-window strategy with an anchor frame maintains global consistency. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that OSVE achieves editing quality comparable or superior to state-of-the-art multi-step methods, while operating approximately 155--171 times faster. This breakthrough paves the way for practical, real-time video editing applications. Code is available at https://github.com/KU-VGI/OSVE.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: one-step video editing with diffusion models for speedup
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 单步视频编辑加速扩散模型推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 单步视频编辑加速扩散模型推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 单步视频编辑加速扩散模型推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: OSVE adapts one-step T2I models for video editing, using a learned encoder to bypass iterative inversion and achieve high-quality editing with temporal consistency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出OSVE框架，首次将单步文本到图像扩散模型成功应用于视频编辑。通过训练可学习编码器替代缓慢的迭代反演，并设计结构感知编辑损失保持几何结构，统一帧编辑确保时间一致性。实验表明，该方法大幅降低视频编辑延迟，同时保持高质量。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出OSVE框架，首次将单步文本到图像扩散模型成功应用于视频编辑。通过训练可学习编码器替代缓慢的迭代反演，并设计结构感知编辑损失保持几何结构，统一帧编辑确保时间一致性。实验表明，该方法大幅降低视频编辑延迟，同时保持高质量。

deep_dive.title: Evolving Cache Schedules for Fast Diffusion Policy Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Diffusion policies achieve strong visuomotor control by iteratively denoising action chunks, but repeated denoising makes real-time deployment computationally demanding. Cache-based methods reduce inference cost by reusing intermediate activations, but existing training-free schedules typically allocate computation uniformly across blocks, ignoring heterogeneous redundancy across blocks and leading to a suboptimal performance-efficiency trade-off. To bridge this gap, we introduce Evolving Cache Schedules (EVO), a training-free acceleration framework that globally schedules cache refreshes via evolutionary search. EVO represents each candidate as a complete schedule over the block-timestep lattice. Thus, redundant transformer computations during iterative denoising can be skipped through cache reuse while preserving closed-loop rollout performance. To make the search practical, EVO introduces redundancy-aware initialization, which seeds the population with promising schedules, and target-conditioned early stopping, which verifies and terminates once a desired performance target is reached. The offline-optimized schedule can be directly plugged into pretrained diffusion policies without retraining. Extensive manipulation benchmarks show that EVO preserves near-full performance while substantially reducing computation, achieving up to 8.05x action-generation speedup and reducing FLOPs from 15.77G to as low as 1.96G. Source code is available at https://github.com/pillom/EVO.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: training-free caching schedule for fast diffusion inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 免训练缓存调度加速扩散推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 免训练缓存调度加速扩散推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 免训练缓存调度加速扩散推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: EVO introduces evolutionary search to automatically discover optimal cache refresh schedules for diffusion policy inference, achieving training-free acceleration by eliminating redundant computation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散策略推理中重复去噪导致计算成本高昂，现有缓存方法均匀分配计算，忽略了不同块之间的异构冗余。EVO提出训练无关的框架，通过进化搜索全局优化缓存刷新调度，在每个块-时间步网格上智能决定何时刷新缓存。实验表明，EVO在保持控制性能的同时显著减少计算量，为扩散模型推理加速提供了通用的调度优化方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 扩散策略推理中重复去噪导致计算成本高昂，现有缓存方法均匀分配计算，忽略了不同块之间的异构冗余。EVO提出训练无关的框架，通过进化搜索全局优化缓存刷新调度，在每个块-时间步网格上智能决定何时刷新缓存。实验表明，EVO在保持控制性能的同时显著减少计算量，为扩散模型推理加速提供了通用的调度优化方法。

quick_skim.title: Multi-Mask Diffusion Language Models for Few-Step Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Masked diffusion models (MDMs) are a promising family of language generators, but achieving high-quality few-step generation remains challenging. In MDMs, all forward trajectories collapse to a single fully masked state, leaving no terminal entropy for consistency-style few-step generation. While recent few-step alternatives based on uniform-state diffusion avoid this degeneracy, it becomes harder to distinguish clean tokens from noise than MDMs, which usually harms modeling quality and training efficiency. In this work, we propose a multi-mask diffusion model (MultiMDM) that preserves the masking structure towards few-step generation. In the forward process, each clean token is first pushed towards a designated mask and then gradually mixes over the mask set. As a result, the backward process has a drafting capability by predicting a designated mask before refining to a clean token. We derive a closed-form ELBO training objective for MultiMDM that supports continual training from pretrained MDMs. In addition, we formulate a purely discrete-state consistency distillation scheme, with a shared-Gumbel coupling to reduce pathwise entropy. Experiments on pretraining and distillation show that MultiMDM provides an effective foundation for principled few-step generation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-mask diffusion for few-step generation accelerates text diffusion models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多掩码扩散用于少步生成，加速文本扩散模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多掩码扩散用于少步生成，加速文本扩散模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多掩码扩散用于少步生成，加速文本扩散模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MultiMDM introduces a multi-mask forward process to preserve masking structure, enabling high-quality few-step generation for masked diffusion language models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对掩码扩散语言模型难以高质量少步生成的问题，本文提出多掩码扩散模型，在正向过程中将每个干净词先推至指定掩码再混合，保留掩码结构用于少步采样。该方法在保持建模质量的同时实现高效生成，在语言生成任务上取得优越性能，为文本扩散模型的加速提供了新途径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对掩码扩散语言模型难以高质量少步生成的问题，本文提出多掩码扩散模型，在正向过程中将每个干净词先推至指定掩码再混合，保留掩码结构用于少步采样。该方法在保持建模质量的同时实现高效生成，在语言生成任务上取得优越性能，为文本扩散模型的加速提供了新途径。
